programmes.show_description: Ancient Icons In The Modern Age: Explore the truth and lies behind ancient Egypt's best known and loved icons, including the Great Sphinx, Tutankhamun and more. (S1, ep 4)
Sometimes, the mysterious unidentified objects people see in the sky touch down and leave physical evidence behind. (S1, ep 6)
The Top Ten Mysterious Devices: Count down the top 10 mysterious devices found around the world, from strange discs found in a cave in China to an ancient Greek computer. (S15, ep 12)
Proving Grounds: Kidd and Harry navigate raging rapids to deliver a scow boat to its new owner. Meanwhile, Tom passes on his expertise to the young buck. (S11, ep 6)
Mad Woman Woods: Troy and Pickle help a friend look for his dog, Bill and Tes search for pythons in a spot ravaged by wildfires, and Dusty hunts in an area with a dark past. (S3, ep 12)
Breakdown at Tiffany's: A man brings in a 160-year-old Tiffany watch. But with no official markings on it, will Les be tempted by his $40,000 asking price? (S10, ep 9)
Join antiques expert and presenter David Dickinson as more collectors try to sell their items to dealers. Will they strike a deal or take a chance at auction instead? (S9, ep 36)
Leek 1: David Dickinson is in Leek, Staffordshire, where items of interest include glass sailing boats and a Rolex watch. (S10, ep 16)
Pawned Lightning: Rick and Corey race to San Diego to take a spin in the classic car Hell's Chariot, from the movie Grease. (S24, ep 6)
Vintage restorers are back, challenging themselves to make money out of buying and selling other people's automotive junk and classic collectables. (S3, ep 9)
The Donut Effect: Darrell finds a sweet way to distract the other bidders. Meanwhile, Jarrod and Brandi plan a four-year anniversary party for their Orange Store. (S6, ep 12)
The Gutfather: Rene gets superstitious as he hopes to strike it lucky for a second time at auctions in Fontana. Ivy goes with his gut. But will his instincts pay off? (S6, ep 17)
Dirty Gold Rush: The buyers lock horns to win a unit packed with valuable construction equipment, before Brandon and Lori spot something that no one else sees. (S1, ep 13)
Native Wars: Minds are blown when two buyers team up for a major score. It's an unlikely pair that, together, could make a pile of cash. (S1, ep 14)
Blow a Casket: The Israelis form an unexpected alliance that threatens to bury their competition. (S1, ep 13)
Blowing Smoke: When a container with a shady past lands on the auction block, Mo and Ty prepare themselves to go all in. (S1, ep 14)
The Summit: Mike climbs Kodiak Island's highest peaks to take down a mountain goat, while Martha and Elli use their tracked vehicle to scout new hunting grounds. (S11, ep 7)
The Dead Zone: Bill and Tes battle the Everglades heat as Troy and Pickle search for a python paradise. Meanwhile, a special day for Dusty goes awry. (S3, ep 13)
Put a Cork in It: A seller comes in with a poster by Henri de Toulouse-Lautrec, Chum unboxes some 1912 Yale baseball uniforms and Rick looks at a ukulele from 1919. (S24, ep 5)
Grin And Barry It: Barry sets out to humiliate Ivy in the next set of auctions. Rene is in for a nasty surprise, while Jarrod and Brandi find themselves surrounded by pigs. (S6, ep 14)
The Adventures of Max Profit: Ivy is on the hunt for high-end items in Moreno Valley, while Jarrod reveals to Brandi an alter ego known as Max Profit. (S6, ep 15)
Episode 8: Henry and Skid visit moped fan Dave, scoop up fresh treasures, brave bad weather and chase more finds at FM Hutchings before selling for profit. (S4, ep 8)
The Top Ten Mysterious Islands: Count down the world's top 10 mysterious islands, including sites said to be home to giants and islands that disappear into thin air. (S15, ep 13)
The Roswell Rock: Ancient Aliens' Giorgio Tsoukalos investigates whether aliens lie at the heart of Earth's most famous mysteries, beginning with the enigma of Roswell Rock. (S1, ep 1)
Dirty Gold Rush: The buyers lock horns to win a unit packed with valuable construction equipment, before Brandon and Lori spot something that no one else sees. (S1, ep 13)
Native Wars: Minds are blown when two buyers team up for a major score. It's an unlikely pair that, together, could make a pile of cash. (S1, ep 14)
The Donut Effect: Darrell finds a sweet way to distract the other bidders. Meanwhile, Jarrod and Brandi plan a four-year anniversary party for their Orange Store. (S6, ep 12)
The Gutfather: Rene gets superstitious as he hopes to strike it lucky for a second time at auctions in Fontana. Ivy goes with his gut. But will his instincts pay off? (S6, ep 17)
Leek 2: Leek in Staffordshire hosts David Dickinson and his team, featuring a Minton vase and a 1920s beaded bag from the Flapper era. (S10, ep 17)
Episode 8: Henry and Skid visit moped fan Dave, scoop up fresh treasures, brave bad weather and chase more finds at FM Hutchings before selling for profit. (S4, ep 8)
Gold Fever: A crew of car lovers at a garage in the Rocky Mountains transforms abandoned heaps of rust into collectible classics. (S5, ep 7)
Not On My Street: In San Diego, an enraged dog walker goes ballistic when she confronts a speeding driver in her neighbourhood. (S1, ep 7)
Attack of the Tacos: A man is pursued by an angry driver wielding a finger gun. A driver raging at two women in California is intercepted by two men who send him packing. (S1, ep 8)
Pirates in the Caribbean: Are modern pirates prowling the Bermuda Triangle in search of victims? The team investigates the case of a luxury boat lost in 1974. (S2, ep 8)
They keep coming back. There are areas around the world that seem to keep attracting UFOs, where objects return to the skies again and again, as if taunting witnesses below. (S1, ep 7)
The Top Ten Mysterious Islands: Count down the world's top 10 mysterious islands, including sites said to be home to giants and islands that disappear into thin air. (S15, ep 13)
The Summit: Mike climbs Kodiak Island's highest peaks to take down a mountain goat, while Martha and Elli use their tracked vehicle to scout new hunting grounds. (S11, ep 7)
The Dead Zone: Bill and Tes battle the Everglades heat as Troy and Pickle search for a python paradise. Meanwhile, a special day for Dusty goes awry. (S3, ep 13)
Harold's Gamble: Les offers to buy a diamond-encrusted Audemars Piguet watch from a customer but asks his watch expert take a closer look at it first. (S10, ep 11)
Join antiques expert and presenter David Dickinson as more collectors try to sell their items to dealers. Will they strike a deal or take a chance at auction instead? (S9, ep 37)
Leek 2: Leek in Staffordshire hosts David Dickinson and his team, featuring a Minton vase and a 1920s beaded bag from the Flapper era. (S10, ep 17)
Blast From the Past: A customer walks in with a Tommy Boy poster signed by Chris Farley. Meanwhile, Rick tries not to slip up when making a deal on a pair of snowshoes. (S24, ep 7)
Episode 8: Henry and Skid visit moped fan Dave, scoop up fresh treasures, brave bad weather and chase more finds at FM Hutchings before selling for profit. (S4, ep 8)
Grin And Barry It: Barry sets out to humiliate Ivy in the next set of auctions. Rene is in for a nasty surprise, while Jarrod and Brandi find themselves surrounded by pigs. (S6, ep 14)
The Adventures of Max Profit: Ivy is on the hunt for high-end items in Moreno Valley, while Jarrod reveals to Brandi an alter ego known as Max Profit. (S6, ep 15)
Smoking Hot: Brandon and Jesse lock horns in a bidding war. One of them spies an authentic treasure hidden among the contents, but only one will walk away with the score. (S1, ep 15)
Triple X: Our buyers bid big to win a locker packed with what looks like rare art, but will the winner walk away with cash or counterfeits? (S1, ep 16)
Cargo Contraband-O: The competition approaches fever pitch when a container from south of the border lands at the Port of Los Angeles. (S1, ep 15)
Lost at Sea: The biggest auction on the planet sails to the middle of the Pacific Ocean when a mysterious container is dredged up from the bottom of the sea. (S1, ep 16)
Winter Kill: Kidd and Harry use explosives to battle a deep freeze in order to protect their cattle. Meanwhile, Jake's young pups learn a valuable lesson in hunting. (S11, ep 8)
Snakin' Ain't Easy: Troy and Pickle help rid a citrus grove of pythons, Bill is forced to hunt solo for the day, and Bruce and Zak mount an airboat to a new honey hole. (S3, ep 14)
Pawned Lightning: Rick and Corey race to San Diego to take a spin in the classic car Hell's Chariot, from the movie Grease. (S24, ep 6)
Follow all the drama as the buyers bid on more abandoned storage lockers. What treasures lie inside and can the dealers sell them on for a profit? (S6, ep 16)
Pay The Dan: Ivy gets excited by the prospect of high-end lockers. Rene and Casey struggle to agree and Darrell tries to distract his rivals with some old-fashioned pranks. (S6, ep 17)
Vintage restorers are back, challenging themselves to make money out of buying and selling other people's automotive junk and classic collectables. (S3, ep 10)
Top Ten Alien Craft: Count down the top ten alien craft, from V-shaped objects to orbs of light to flying saucers. (S15, ep 14)
The Hunt For Atlantis: New clues narrow down the possible location of Atlantis and suggest that Plato's mysterious island was the home of an advanced civilisation. (S1, ep 2)
Smoking Hot: Brandon and Jesse lock horns in a bidding war. One of them spies an authentic treasure hidden among the contents, but only one will walk away with the score. (S1, ep 15)
Triple X: Our buyers bid big to win a locker packed with what looks like rare art, but will the winner walk away with cash or counterfeits? (S1, ep 16)
Grin And Barry It: Barry sets out to humiliate Ivy in the next set of auctions. Rene is in for a nasty surprise, while Jarrod and Brandi find themselves surrounded by pigs. (S6, ep 14)
The Adventures of Max Profit: Ivy is on the hunt for high-end items in Moreno Valley, while Jarrod reveals to Brandi an alter ego known as Max Profit. (S6, ep 15)
Leek 3: David is in Leek and amongst the items brought in are a map that belonged to a French general in the Crimean War and a striking Bernard Moore vase. (S10, ep 18)
An Oregon woman doing some work on a remote cabin is spied on by a dark figure. Next, a man in Arkansas encounters something terrifying in the woods. (S8, ep 15)
In Florida, an investigator captures dark shadow figures while walking through a cemetery and a young girl in Ohio sees the ghost of her late grandfather. (S8, ep 16)
The UFO Files: In this special, Tony Harris reviews the latest developments and most compelling photographic evidence from a tumultuous year of UFO revelations. (S4, ep 8)
